summary: High‑quality matrix recording captures a solid 1983 show with standout Brokedown Palace, Terrapin Station and an inspired Space jam.
review: The matrix SBD source delivers clear, well‑balanced sound that lets the band’s late‑'80s energy shine through. Reviewers praise the deep jams – especially the post‑drums Space segment and strong renditions of Brokedown Palace, Terrapin Station, Saint Stephen, Far From Me and Good Lovin'. While Jerry only sings a couple of numbers, his guitar work and Bob Weir’s vocal presence are highlighted as highlights.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers rate the show 4‑5 stars and describe both the performance and the matrix recording positively; the weighting of a strong musical showing (70%) and good technical quality (30%) yields an overall rating in the high‑four range.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Nice matrix SBD with solid, high‑quality sound; clear mix despite being a combination source, no major dropouts reported.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The show moves smoothly through two sets, featuring a jam‑heavy second set with a memorable post‑drums Space segment and strong song transitions that keep the energy high.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Sings only two songs but delivers strong guitar work; highlighted on Brokedown Palace, Terrapin Station and in the post‑drums space jam.
band_member_comments.Bob: Bob Weir’s vocal contributions dominate; his songs and a “Bobby fest” are repeatedly praised as highlights of the performance.
